summary: The Son Heung-min show — but a camp under a cloud. Hong Myung-bo's unpopular appointment turned toxic after heavy defeats (5-0 Brazil, 4-0 Ivory Coast, 1-0 Austria), all using a new back three. Breezed through qualifying in a 4-2-3-1; the late switch threatens to derail them. Most dangerous on the counter, when Son and Lee Kang-in are released into space.
style: Experimenting with a back three/five meant to let Son and Lee Kang-in stay high and break forward, with extra cover to absorb pressure. In practice the wing-backs have looked unsure when to drop and when to push, leaving the midfield pivot stretched. Qualifying was a more settled 4-2-3-1.

counterReliance.note: Tournament threat is the counter — Son & Lee Kang-in dangerous in transition; can also dominate weaker sides on the ball

strengths: Son Heung-min — elite finisher and the focal point of everything
Genuine counter-attacking threat with runners in behind
Can control possession and attack vertically against lesser opponents
weaknesses: Back-three switch lacks cohesion and time to bed in before the opener
Wing-backs misjudge their positioning; midfield gets outnumbered and stretched
Defensive disorganisation badly exposed by Brazil, Ivory Coast and Austria

keyPlayers.name: Son Heung-min
keyPlayers.note: Carries the nation at 33. Since 2018: 109 league goals from 81.9 xG — ~27 above an average finisher

keyPlayers.name: Lee Kang-in
keyPlayers.note: The other release valve in transition — dangerous running into open space alongside Son

tactics: The intent behind the back three is sound — keep the attackers high, sit deep, spring forward — but a back-three system needs familiarity South Korea haven't yet built. Hong frames it as Plan A/B versatility depending on the opponent. If the structure holds they counter dangerously; if it's played through, the gaps are large. Group A looks exceptionally tight.
notes: Hong (57) was Bronze Ball winner as third-best player at the 2002 World Cup; his appointment over Klinsmann drew a National Assembly hearing.
Training at altitude in Salt Lake City to prepare for Mexico's high venues.
sources: The Athletic — 2026 World Cup tactical guide, Group A (5 Jun 2026)
John Walker (@johnwalker_1986) — pre-tournament tactical scouting, May–Jun 2026
